closureRule: A gap cannot move directly from IMPLEMENTING to CLOSED.

gaps.businessConsequence: Paid checkout can grant the wrong entitlement or quarantine a valid payment if catalogue code, entitlement slug and Stripe identifiers disagree.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Paid report and governed product checkout
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix filtered by productCode, entitlementSlug, stripePriceId, priceCode, resolvePaymentIdentity, grantEntitlement.
failureModes: mismatched product code and entitlement slug
gaps.currentImplementationState: Checkout metadata normalization and resolver compatibility are locally implemented and tested.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Revert commit fix(commercial): normalise payment and entitlement identity.

gaps.businessConsequence: Duplicate or out-of-order Stripe events can duplicate entitlement or fulfilment records.
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix filtered by processCheckoutCompleted, resolvePaymentIdentity, checkoutSessionId, stripeSessionId.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Existing idempotency records and upserts exist, but adversarial route-level duplicate/out-of-order proof is incomplete.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Disable affected webhook handler through route-level maintenance gate and process event manually from Stripe dashboard.

gaps.businessConsequence: A customer may pay and receive access before required delivery operations are complete, or fulfilment may be marked complete prematurely.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Paid report fulfilment, Boardroom Brief, Executive Reporting, Strategy Room
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix filtered by fulfilment, fulfillment, productCode, entitlementSlug.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Some products have durable fulfilment, others are manual or stubbed; terminal-state invariant is not globally enforced.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Block checkout for affected products with commercial governance state and fulfil manually.

gaps.businessConsequence: Public free signal could leak paid dossier fields, internal graph data or checkout controls.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Free diagnostic and Foundry public tests
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix filtered by kernel-signal and runDecisionIntelligence.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Route-level static contract guards and integrity script are locally passing.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Revert public route changes and disable new public persistence adapter.

gaps.businessConsequence: The business may believe a public signal was durably stored when it only exists in memory.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Free diagnostic persistence and repeat-session continuity
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix filtered by DiagnosticJourney, DiagnosticJourneyEvent, caseId, persistJourney.
failureModes: memory-only fallback reported as durable success
gaps.currentImplementationState: Public-safe adapter exists and store attempts Prisma creation; durable-vs-memory success semantics still need explicit runtime contract and database integration proof.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Set public persistence to no-op and preserve free signal response path.

gaps.businessConsequence: One anonymous case or user could read another case's events.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Repeat public diagnostic and future accumulated-depth reads
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix filtered by caseId and DiagnosticJourney.
failureModes: same case ID with different identity
different case IDs same anonymous session
gaps.currentImplementationState: No graph accumulation has started; isolation tests are still required before any read path is added.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Disable cross-request memory reads and serve single-request signal only.

gaps.businessConsequence: The compounding decision authority claim remains weak if accumulated depth is not derived from authorised safe events.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Repeat free diagnostic and paid upgrade qualification
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix filtered by DiagnosticJourney, caseId, kernel-signal, runDecisionIntelligence.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Not started by instruction. Public-safe event base now exists for future implementation.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Remove accumulated-depth field and ignore historical events.

gaps.businessConsequence: The product cannot prove efficacy or calibrate predictions without verified outcomes tied to exact decision/action/deadline.
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix filtered by OutcomeVerification and outcome verification.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Outcome infrastructure exists but public signal route does not yet capture outcome verification.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Disable public outcome capture while preserving existing paid outcome services.

gaps.businessConsequence: Split Pages/App route ownership creates inconsistent behaviour and unclear external webhook registration.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Public diagnostics, checkout, webhooks, Foundry, Strategy Room
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix routeOrWorker fields for pages_api_route and app_api_route.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Router migration is deferred by instruction. Canonical billing route remains Pages Router.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Keep legacy shim and revert single-domain migration commit.

gaps.businessConsequence: Public-safe records may outlive policy or fail deletion across linked tables.
gaps.authoritativeSource: DiagnosticJourney, DiagnosticEvidenceNode, public signal persistence policy
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix filtered by DiagnosticJourney, caseId, OutcomeVerification.
gaps.currentImplementationState: No retention/deletion policy implementation is proven for public-safe signal records.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Suspend durable public persistence and delete affected public-safe journeys manually.

gaps.businessConsequence: Failures can recur silently without structured events, alerts, and release gates.
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix for payment, persistence, outcome and fulfilment terms.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Telemetry names and alert requirements are defined in this ledger; implementation not complete.
telemetryRequirement: all events listed in programme section 7
gaps.rollbackMethod: Disable affected product checkout or public persistence via governance gate.

gaps.businessConsequence: Internal unit tests may pass while real route boundaries fail.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Free diagnostic, paid report, failure recovery, outcome loop
gaps.authoritativeSource: Playwright E2E and route-level integration suites
gaps.allConsumers: See consumer matrix route consumers and tests.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Focused local suites pass; required E2E golden paths are not yet implemented.
gaps.rollbackMethod: Block release until E2E failures are resolved or scoped exception is approved.

failureModes: engine selected by name not authority
gaps.businessConsequence: Teams cannot know which engine owns decision authority, making claims, tests and rollout sequencing unsafe.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Confirmed ambiguity; target layered architecture selected but not implemented.
gaps.allConsumers: All consumers in decision-engine authority matrix

failureModes: DecisionIntelligenceKernel persists living case while public-safe event path persists separate journey
gaps.businessConsequence: A request may create parallel decision records that disagree or cannot be reconciled.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Public Free Signal and paid diagnostic escalation
gaps.rollbackMethod: Disable new public adapter work and keep free-signal persistence only.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Confirmed risk in public route; single-record contract added, enforcement incomplete.
gaps.allConsumers: Public signal route and persistence adapter

failureModes: graph depth claimed without authorised prior state
one-off output marketed as compounding memory
gaps.businessConsequence: One-off kernel calls may be mistaken for accumulated graph operation.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Executive reporting, Strategy Room, instruments, future public graph
requiredTests: existingGraph provenance required before accumulated-depth claims
gaps.currentImplementationState: Confirmed; graph accumulation explicitly not started.

gaps.businessConsequence: Pages/App Router divergence can create security, cache, privacy or behavioural inconsistencies.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Public signal, diagnostics, executive reporting, Strategy Room
gaps.currentImplementationState: Router audit complete with zero UNKNOWN; customer-critical behavioural gaps remain.
gaps.allConsumers: Pages Router and App Router API routes

failureModes: policy helper treated as durable store
gaps.businessConsequence: SharedMemoryBridge could be wired as authority without persistence, tenancy or deletion proof.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Memory progression and future graph accumulation
gaps.rollbackMethod: Leave bridge unused until authoritative store contract exists.
requiredTests: consent/no raw storage tests before use
gaps.currentImplementationState: Classified UNUSED; do not wire merely because it exists.

failureModes: dual write required for durable acknowledgement
gaps.businessConsequence: Introducing hot-state storage prematurely can create dual-write truth and cache-loss data loss.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Idempotency, rate limiting, graph accumulation, public signal persistence
gaps.rollbackMethod: Do not introduce Redis in Phase 1.5.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Redis need not proven; Prisma remains proposed durable authority.

failureModes: automation based on price instead of deterministic fulfilment
gaps.businessConsequence: Duplicate abstractions make features slower and can create parallel product truth.
gaps.affectedCustomerJourney: Paid product fulfilment and diagnostic surfaces
gaps.rollbackMethod: Keep founder review until eligibility gates pass.
gaps.currentImplementationState: Confirmed risk; automation eligibility ledger added.
gaps.allConsumers: Product catalogue, surface registry, fulfilment, gold composers
